summary: Support Tickets feature tested end-to-end (backend + frontend). 15/15 pytest cases passed. All critical UX flows verified via Playwright: login → sidebar nav → /tickets kanban (73 cards) → table (73 rows) → search → mine-only toggle → create → detail modal → note add → delete. Scorecard widget renders counts correctly (73 Open / 72 Overdue / 0 Resolved 7d) and links to /tickets. Slack urgent notifications fire on both new-urgent creation and escalation, with idempotency verified. Tally webhook rejects wrong form IDs, creates tickets on D4BW1N, and is idempotent on duplicate submissionId. Sync is idempotent (second call inserts 0). Non-admin coach user blocked with 403 as expected.
critical_code_review_comments: tickets.py: is_overdue uses created_at as the start of SLA clock; consider resetting when status moves to waiting or assignee changes (not a bug, but may inflate overdue counts for tickets stuck in 'waiting' legitimately).

routes/tickets.py line 123-124: setting resolved_at=None for ANY non-resolved status means moving closed→open also clears resolved_at. Current behaviour matches the spec ('reverting away clears it') but verify this is intended for the closed status path too.
routes/tickets.py tally_webhook is intentionally public — acceptable per spec (Phase 1). For Phase 2 consider HMAC signing via Tally's signing secret header.
tickets.py _tally_fetch_submissions caps at 5 pages * 100 = 500 submissions. Fine for 73 current, but consider pagination-until-empty or watermarking by submittedAt for long-term scaling.
seed_data_creation: No seed data created. Test tickets created during the run were all cleaned up via DELETE. 73 Tally-imported tickets remain intact.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/test_tickets.py covers list, stats, CRUD, filters, search, notes, Tally sync idempotency, Tally webhook (wrong formId rejected, D4BW1N accepted + idempotent), permissions. Slack webhook is real (hits hooks.slack.com) — running test suite repeatedly WILL post to Slack each time an urgent ticket is created or escalated. Total of 2 real Slack posts per full run (one for create-urgent, one for escalation). Stat card testid prefix is 'ticket-stat-' (singular) not 'tickets-stat-'.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. Feature works as specified.
